pattern: Walking the list one step at a time and splicing a brand-new node (holding the gcd of the current node and the one right after it) between them, then hopping past both the original next node and the freshly inserted one, threads the new nodes in without ever losing track of the rest of the list.
hints: You only ever need to look at one node and its immediate neighbor at a time -- there is no need to know anything about the rest of the list beyond that pair.
For a pair of adjacent nodes, compute the greatest common divisor of their two values and splice a new node holding that value in between them.

Advance a pointer through the list; at each step, before moving to the original next node, insert the new gcd node and repoint so the new node's next is the original next node, then continue from that original next node.

edge_inputs.description: a chain of powers of two with a clean gcd cascade
two nodes at the maximum value, identical
every adjacent pair is coprime so every inserted value is one
each value evenly divides the previous one
a long run of identical values

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    vals = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [vals]
